blocks.text: DTC P0335: Crank sensor No pulse

The crank sensor consists of a pulsar rotor and a semiconductor that detects the rotor position. When the engine starts, the pulsar rotor rotates and the magnetic flux of the crank sensor fluctuates. The magnetic flux variation is converted into a pulse signal and sent to the PGM-FI ECU. The crank sensor is used to control the ignition timing and fuel injection timing of each cylinder. If no pulse signal is input from the crank sensor, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
At least 2 seconds (at 750 rpm engine speed)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed

blocks.text: When no pulse signal from the crank sensor is input while the pulse signal from the TDC sensor is detected 75 times or more.

items.text: Disconnection of cord (CRKN line) between PGM-FI ECU and crank sensor
Ground fault in cord (CRKN line) between PGM-FI ECU and crank sensor
Disconnection of crank sensor cord (power supply line)
Disconnection of cord (CRKSG line) between PGM-FI ECU and crank sensor

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
